name: Charred Habanero Salsa
recipeIngredient: 1/8 teaspoon coarse salt, or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a dry comal or flat iron griddle over moderately low heat until hot.
2. Pan-roast the tomatoes and habaneros, turning them occasionally for even roasting, until they are browned and soft throughout (about 25-30 minutes).
3. Remove tomato stems and, wearing rubber gloves, stem and seed the chiles.
4. In a blender or food processor, purée the tomatoes, chiles, water, and salt until smooth.
5. The salsa can be made up to 10 hours in advance and chilled, covered. Bring the salsa to room temperature before serving.
Makes about 1 cup.
